title: Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive
description: Transmitting sensitive data over clear-text protocols (such as HTTP, FTP, or Telnet) exposes it to interception, eavesdropping, and manipulation by attackers. All sensitive communications should use encrypted protocols (such as HTTPS, SFTP, or SSH) to ensure confidentiality and integrity.
message: Clear-text protocol detected. Use encrypted protocols for all sensitive communications.
impact: Clear-text protocols allow attackers to intercept and read sensitive data, leading to data breaches, credential theft, and compliance violations.
recommendation: Replace all clear-text protocols with their encrypted counterparts. For example, use HTTPS instead of HTTP, SFTP instead of FTP, and SSH instead of Telnet. Update resource configurations and security groups accordingly.

noncompliant.description: Sensitive data is transmitted over HTTP, a clear-text protocol.

compliant.description: Sensitive data is transmitted over HTTPS, an encrypted protocol.

devsecops.security: Protects data in transit from interception and tampering.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD pipelines should scan for clear-text protocol usage and block deployments if found.
devsecops.compliance: Meets regulatory requirements for secure data transmission (e.g., PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR).

why_is_this_an_issue: Clear-text protocols expose sensitive data to anyone with network access, making it easy for attackers to steal credentials, personal information, or confidential business data.
how_to_fix: Audit all resource configurations and scripts for clear-text protocol usage. Replace with encrypted protocols and test to ensure secure communication.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
security_mappings.CERT: MSC61-J: Do not use insecure or weak cryptographic algorithms
